THE 2026 LICC IS NOW OPEN FOR SUBMISSIONS!

Submit your most creative work by July 21 and receive 20% off your entry fee.

SUBMIT NOW

The London International Creative Competition Winners

We are thrilled to announce that our eminent judging panel has chosen the Best In Category winners of the 2025 Edition of the London International Creative Competition.

Thousands of ingenious submissions from all around the world were submitted to our world-recognized jury members, making the selection process more challenging than ever. The winners have outperformed expectations in every category, bringing both authenticity and uniqueness to the table.

The winners of the Professional and Non-Professional categories will earn €1,500 and €1,000 in cash, respectively, as well as the LICC trophy and the title of “Best in Category.”

Congratulations to all LICC winners!

VIEW 2025 WINNERS
SUBMIT NOW
London International Creative Competition is back for a new, fresh and creative edition!

Our mission is clear: recognising and rewarding the world’s most fresh and progressive creative talents, from all around the world, in different categories – Architecture, Photo/Video, Art, Graphic, Interior, and Product Design.

Talented creatives, designers, photographers, architects, and artists around the world are invited to enter the award, for a chance to take their work global and connect with the brightest design minds in the industry.

ABOUT THE AWARDS

We want to see vision. We want to see ambition. We want to see intuition.
Photo: "Léo Armchair Atelier" by Chico Margarita
CATEGORIES

LICC provides opportunity to all creative minds in visual arts and design, with 6 Main Categories and dozens of Sub-Categories to cover all segments of creative art. Click on the list to explore all categories!

BUILD (Architecture)
CREATE (Art)
ILLUSTRATE (Graphic)
DECORATE (Interior)
SHOOT (Photo/Video)
USE (Product)
ENTER NOW

The LICC is a way for the visionaries to really stand out from the crowd. We’re here to help cultivate creativity, to help artists, designers and creatives push boundaries and break down walls. The LICC isn’t here to plant seeds, but to help them grow. We’re keen to hear from people who look at life a little differently, who fit squares into circles and for whom the ordinary can be truly, truly extraordinary.

LICC https://licc.uk/ London International Creative Competition Tue, 09 Jul 2024 11:59:21 +0000 en-US hourly 1 https://wordpress.org/?v=6.9.4 https://licc.uk/wp-content/uploads/2018/12/favicon-150x150.png LICC https://licc.uk/ 32 32 The Top Graphic Designers from the London International Creative Competition https://licc.uk/2024/07/09/the-top-graphic-designers-from-the-london-international-creative-competition/ https://licc.uk/2024/07/09/the-top-graphic-designers-from-the-london-international-creative-competition/#respond Tue, 09 Jul 2024 11:59:21 +0000 https://licc.uk/?p=2920 The post The Top Graphic Designers from the London International Creative Competition appeared first on LICC.

]]>

In order to hook in a client and make your product pop, good graphic design is a necessity. Whether through illustration, digital art, or photography, a strong aesthetic vision goes hand in hand with a good product. Without this necessary component, a truly special product can be totally ignored. See the top graphic designs from the 2023 LICC winners below and how they were able to elevate their products and ideas!

Keep on Walking by Fa-Hsiang Hu

In this advertisement for socks, illustrator Fa-Hsiang Hu takes a creative approach. By creating a cityscape in the shape of the sock, their design shows the integrity and strength of the product.

Fudo Labo Collection by Hajime Tsushima

Created for a Japanese sake brewery, Tsushima is able to blend contemporary art with a traditional product. The beautiful modern design is able to elevate a simple drink to a work of art.

Limoncito Magazine by Jessica Martinez

In this prototype for her magazine, Martinez blends Mexican design aesthetic with food. Her use of color and pattern pops and creates a unique style unlike other magazines.

Cover Design Of Textbooks—School of Art Design and Media, ECUST by Ziyin Qu

In this design for a 5 volume textbook, Ziyin Qu creates a seamless design of buildings. Not only does this help to create a cohesive design, it also mirrors the layout of the college the textbook is made for.

2024 Tamdhu Year of the Dragon by Kyo Chen

Created for the Chinese year of the dragon, this bottle design is traditional yet new. It adheres to classic representation of the Chinese dragon with modern elements.

SinkinSync by Xin Feng

This graphic design for VR blends all the ideas of the product with what the consumer desires. By creating this futuristic design, Xin Feng represents what people want from VR, futuristic technology in the present.

Who is eligible?

The LICC competition is open to anyone aged 18+ residing anywhere in the world. Professional entries will be judged against other professionals and student entries against other students.

Submission Guidelines

Entrants must submit their work online. An image can be a JPEG (.jpeg, .jpg) or PNG (.png). One image is for the cover photo to identify the entry and the remaining 9 are supporting images of the entry. Images must not exceed 4MB per file and a minimum of 1000 pixels wide. Please only use letters and numbers in the title of the image.

If your entry has a product specification, you have an option to upload one PDF file in A4 or lettersized format. The PDF must not exceed 4MB.

Who is considered professional?

Designers who earn, or have earned, the majority of their income from design.

Entry Forms and Submissions

Please note that no entry will be processed unless the entry fee has been received. Work submitted without proper forms and payment will be disqualified. After payment has been made, entries cannot be altered.

Payments and Refunds

Once a submission fee has been paid, there are no refunds provided. If you have been wrongly charged for any reason, we will be happy to credit the amount that is due.

Categories

You may enter your work in as many categories as you see fit. However, the LICC jury and staff will not switch your entry into a different category on your behalf.

Use and Ownership of Materials

Copyright and all other rights remain with the designer. Any images used by LICC shall carry the designer’s credit line. LICC may use images in any media sponsor publication. All entrants understand that any image submitted to the competition may be used by LICC for marketing and promotional purposes including in any medium such as exhibitions, print, and digital media directly related to the LICC competition though there may not be monetary compensation. By winning or placing in the competition, you are agreeing to be included in the winner’s annual book.

We want to make paradigms a thing of the past. We want to see tomorrow’s designs today.
Photo: "Meizhou Academician Hall" by Jumbo Globe Limited, Nan Ni
LONDON INTERNATIONAL CREATIVE COMPETITION

Since starting out in 2006, the London International Creative Competition has been recognising and rewarding the world’s most fresh and progressive creative talents. Our artists and designers are connected not by conventions, but by qualities. They work across a diverse range of formats, genres and industries, creating stunning and essential works of art in their respective fields. We’re not looking for anything done by-the-book. We’re looking for work that is as visual as it is vital.

About Us

LICC is the brainchild of two artists, Launa Bacon and Hossein Farmani, for Farmani Group.

Farmani Group is a leading organization curating and promoting photography, design and architecture across the globe since 1985. Hossein Farmani is the founder of two non-profit organizations “The Lucie Awards” and “Focus on AIDS” as well as the award-winning VUE magazine, International Photography Awards, Px3 (Prix de la Photographie de Paris), Art For New York, aNet Communications and more.

Winning LICC is an exciting opportunity, and an incredible building block for a career in design and the arts. Every year our panel of established creative luminaries evaluate thousands of exceptional entries across multiple design disciplines. Never has there been such a seamless Segway into the forefront of the global art and design community. LICC brings together the most cutting-edge and innovative artists and designers from around the world onto a single international platform.

The Annual London International Creative Competition winners will receive a cash prizes of €1,500 in the Professional Categories, and €1,000 in the Amateur/Student Categories, along with the Category Title and Trophy in their respective Discipline. 

Why Enter LICC?
Cash Prizes

By entering LICC you have the chance to win a total €10,000 in cash prizes, along with the “Best Of” Category Title, for either the Professional or Amateur/Student division. Finalists and Shortlist Winners will also be awarded.

Career Growth

The unparalleled media exposure worldwide, and a wealth of priceless opportunities can help strengthen your profile and take your professional career to the next level

Renowned Jury

Our jury is made up of internationally esteemed design luminaries, creatives, curators, editors, and other artistic arbiters

Enter your projects into 6 main categories and 70+ sub-categories
Categories
BUILD (Architecture)
CREATE (Art)
ILLUSTRATE (Graphic)
DECORATE (Interior)
SHOOT (Photo/Video)
USE (Product)
VIEW ALL

“What the Soul wears over its new complexion” by Beate Walden Photography


Entry Fees

In order to cover the administration costs of LICC, and to help fund the promotion and publicity of the winners, we do ask for a small entry fee. This is the only fee you will pay. Unlike other Awards, there are no extra or hidden costs if your project wins. Winners’ benefits and assets are included in the entry fee. We do not charge for the use of winners’ seal, certificates or trophies.

BUILD (Architecture)
DECORATE (Interior)
CREATE (Art)
ILLUSTRATE (Graphic)
SHOOT (Photo/Video)
USE (Product)

The standard of submission is exceptionally high! Each year we welcome thousands of entries from creatives from over 80 countries around the world. 

In the first round, the “Official Selection” is made and put through for a second and final round of Jury voting. Votes are tallied with top-scoring entries in each sub-category making the “Shortlist”. From there, up to 20 Finalists are selected in each Category, with a Final Winner chosen to win the Category Title and a total of €10,000 in cash prizes, in the Professional and the Amateur/Student categories. 

All voting is done online and anonymously, to ensure total impartiality and utmost integrity in the jury and winner selection process.

Selection of Previous Winners
Submission Guidelines

We welcome submissions from every corner of the globe, with previous finalists hailing from from Iran to India, from Ecuador to Ireland, and from Italy to the U.S. We don’t care where you’re from; we just care about where you’re going. We want to make this THE year the World learns your name!

We want to hear from both experienced designers and new, exciting artists. We want to be the platform that honours great creativity in all its forms, introducing unique, extraordinary, talents to an international audience.

The submission process is quick and easy. Please take a few seconds to read through our requirements and some submission tips below. You can also find more information and answers to commonly asked questions on our FAQ page.

“what is in the park?” by Lin Ye

Make it happen

For us, art is oxygen. Without it, our planet simply wouldn’t be the same incredible place it is. Imagine a city without architecture. What would it look like?

Without creativity, the world would just be an empty frame.
Without creatives, the world would be monochrome.

Being a creative is about defining your own set of rules, then breaking them.

It’s about recognizing the most difficult path, then taking it.
It’s about walking right off the edge of the map.
